title: mimusops elengi f. longepedunculata
intro: Mimusops elengi f. longepedunculata, also known as the Bullet wood or Bengal quinine, is a variant of the Mimusops elengi tree, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimalarial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like quinine and scopoletin. The health benefits of Mimusops elengi f. longepedunculata include its ability to treat fever, malaria, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
